sentence: Find a non-PFAS substitute for THV terpolymer used as a fuel hose liner that maintains chemical resistance, flexibility, and barrier properties.

required: Chemical resistance to gasoline, diesel, and additives
Processable by extrusion or injection molding
Resistance to fuel‑line contaminants (e.g., lubricants, detergents)
Adequate abrasion resistance for hose lining
Compatibility with other hose materials (e.g., rubber, PTFE liners)
Maintain low friction in the fuel‑line environment
Easy to clean or maintain (no residue buildup)
Proven safety in automotive fuel‑line testing

extracted_keywords: Chemical resistance to gasoline, diesel, and additives
Processable by extrusion or injection molding
Resistance to fuel‑line contaminants (e.g., lubricants, detergents)
Adequate abrasion resistance for hose lining
Compatibility with other hose materials (e.g., rubber, PTFE liners)
Maintain low friction in the fuel‑line environment
Easy to clean or maintain (no residue buildup)
Proven safety in automotive fuel‑line testing
extracted_constraints: PFAS‑free – The material must contain no per‑fluoroalkyl substances; any presence of PFAS would violate regulatory and industry requirements for automotive fuel‑hose components.
Flammability – The material must meet or exceed a UL 94 V‑0 self‑extinguishing rating (or an equivalent automotive flammability standard such as ASTM D 4711 or UNE‑EN 15561).
Processing temperature – The material must be processable at a temperature lower than its melting point so that extrusion or injection molding of the hose does not cause thermal degradation.

Service temperature – The material must sustain continuous service temperatures of at least 120 °C (≈ 248 °F) for gasoline fuel lines and at least 150 °C (≈ 302 °F) for diesel fuel lines without loss of mechanical or barrier properties.

Chemical resistance – The material must resist the typical hydrocarbon fuels (gasoline, diesel) at their normal concentrations, showing no swelling, permeation, or degradation that would compromise the hose’s barrier function.
